blurb: Barrington Hall in Historic Roswell is one of the most beautiful spots in the area, and the Roswell Lavender Festival turns it into something even better for one day. You get a juried fine arts and handmade crafts market set on the shaded historic grounds, surrounded by gardens in peak early-summer form. It is the kind of outing that is easy to make a morning or afternoon of, especially if you combine it with lunch on Canton Street nearby. A short drive from the Perimeter and a genuinely pretty way to spend a Saturday.
scoring_notes: The educated, higher-income Perimeter audience appreciates fine arts markets and historic settings. The Barrington Hall venue adds real appeal. Loses points on uniqueness because arts festivals recur seasonally, and lavender festivals specifically feel like a familiar format. Still a solid inclusion for variety and for the audience segments (empty nesters, adult couples) who genuinely enjoy this type of outing.

blurb: Aurora Cineplex is running a Big Screen Classics series this year, and two of the 12 screenings fall in your week. They are showing on the big screen in luxury heated recliners, which is a genuinely better way to watch an older film than your couch. The specific titles showing this week were not available at press time, so check Aurora's site for the lineup. If you have been meaning to see a classic the way it was meant to be seen, this is a low-effort, high-reward way to spend a Sunday or a Wednesday night.
scoring_notes: The educated, adult-skewing Perimeter audience is a reasonable fit for a classics film series. The luxury recliner format and big-screen classic combination is a step above a typical movie night, which helps. Loses points on uniqueness because it is a recurring monthly series and we do not know the specific film showing this week. Still a solid lower-tier pick for variety in the lineup.

blurb: Roswell Empowered is throwing a Pride Mini Golf Night at The Fringe on Wednesday, and everyone is welcome. It is a casual, fun mid-week event to celebrate Pride Week in Roswell with mini golf, good company, and a welcoming crowd. The Fringe is a newer addition to the Roswell scene and worth checking out if you have not been. Low stakes, high fun, and a nice way to cap the week. Check Roswell Empowered's page for ticket details and start time.
scoring_notes: The Perimeter audience skews younger and more urban-adjacent than East Cobb, which makes this event a reasonable fit. Pride events during Pride Week have natural urgency. Mini golf keeps it accessible and casual rather than niche. Slightly outside the Perimeter core (Roswell), but close enough. Loses a point on demographic fit because the audience is mixed and the event is more niche than the top picks.

time: Sat 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM
blurb: The 27th Annual Flying Colors Butterfly Festival is back at Chattahoochee Nature Center, and it is one of those events that genuinely delivers for everyone in the car. Walk through a live butterfly encounter, meet pollinator experts, and take part in the Migration Game spread across the whole festival. It runs both Saturday and Sunday, so there is plenty of time to fit it in. Families with younger kids will love it. Adults who appreciate the outdoors and a little learning will too. It is a short drive from the Perimeter and one of the better warm-weather traditions in the area.
scoring_notes: A recurring annual festival, which keeps uniqueness moderate. But 27 years in means it is an established, reliable community event rather than a throwaway. The Perimeter has a mix of young families and adults, and this event works for both. Chattahoochee Nature Center is a beloved local institution. Scores well enough to include, especially given the weekend-long run and the live butterfly encounter as a genuine draw.

blurb: If you have walked past Spruill Gallery a hundred times and never stopped in, this Saturday is the one to finally do it. The gallery is hosting a live meet-the-artist event for their current exhibition, "Returns," where you can actually talk to the people who made the work on the walls. It is the kind of thing that turns a passive gallery visit into a real conversation. The Perimeter has a serious arts scene, and Spruill is the heart of it. Free to attend, no pretense required. Worth an hour of your Saturday.
scoring_notes: High education level (70% with a bachelor's or higher) and solid household income suggest this audience engages with arts and culture. The artist engagement format is meaningfully different from just seeing an exhibit, which bumps uniqueness. Spruill Gallery is directly in the coverage area, which is a plus. Scores a bit lower on audience match because it is a niche draw compared to the block party or World Cup event.

blurb: Sunday plans just got better. Chef Michelle Pellegri of Roswell Baking Company is teaching a hands-on Neapolitan pizza making class, and it is the kind of afternoon that is genuinely fun whether you cook a lot or not at all. You will learn the technique, make the dough, build the pizza, and eat what you made. It is a short drive from the Perimeter and a great pick for a date night, a friend group outing, or just something different to do on a Sunday. Spots are limited, so if this sounds like your kind of thing, do not wait.
scoring_notes: The Perimeter audience skews educated, well-paid, and experience-oriented. Hands-on cooking classes are a strong fit for young professionals and couples without kids, and the Neapolitan style gives it a slightly elevated, foodie-adjacent angle. Not a Perimeter-area venue (it is in Roswell), but close enough and specific enough to be worth including. Loses a point on uniqueness because cooking classes recur, but this is a specific instructor and style.

blurb: Friday night in Dunwoody is about to get loud. The Funwoody Restaurant Courtyard is hosting a full-on block party with DJ Fernando spinning a live video music mix of your favorite 80s, 90s, and disco hits. Think outdoor bar energy, great food from the surrounding restaurants, and a crowd that actually knows every word to every song. It is sponsored by the DHS Class of '86, which tells you exactly what kind of night this will be. Free to attend, walkable if you live nearby, and the perfect way to kick off a weekend. Go find your dancing shoes.
scoring_notes: Median age of 38 means this audience grew up with 80s and 90s music. The Perimeter skews more adult than family, so a Friday night outdoor dance party is a strong fit. Funwoody is a well-known local gathering spot. The nostalgia angle and outdoor block party format give it good energy. Loses a point on uniqueness because DJ block parties do recur, but the specific format and sponsor make this one feel like an event rather than a routine night out.

event_name: World Cup Kickoff Party on The Lawn
blurb: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is finally here, and it is literally being played in your backyard. Atlanta is a host city, and this Saturday Ashford Lane is throwing a full-on celebration to kick things off on The Lawn. Kids activities, soccer experiences, food, and the energy of a city that knows it has a front-row seat to the biggest sporting event on the planet. This is not just a typical watch party. It is the start of something that only happens once every four years, and this time the world is coming to us. Free to attend, right in the neighborhood, and the kind of afternoon you will want to be part of. Get out there.
scoring_notes: The 2026 World Cup is a once-in-a-generation event hosted in Atlanta. This kickoff party is hyper-local (Ashford Lane is literally in the Perimeter coverage area), free, and appeals to the full audience mix: young professionals, young families, and sports fans of all ages. Uniqueness score is a 10 because the World Cup coming to Atlanta is a historic moment and this is the official community celebration of it.

event_name: Global Running Day: Run, Walk & Social at Brook Run Park
blurb:
Global Running Day is June 4th, and Dunwoody is celebrating it a day early with Big Peach Running Co. at Brook Run Park. It's a run, a walk, and a social after, all paces and abilities welcome.

Brook Run is a great park and this is a genuinely easy yes: it's free, it's in the neighborhood, it's midweek, and you end with people. The social part is the whole reason to go.

Show up, move your body, meet someone new. Wednesday nights could use more of this.
scoring_notes: Active, health-conscious crowd at 38 median age is a natural fit for a running event. Dunwoody is in the coverage area. Free and low-friction. The Global Running Day tie-in gives it a reason to exist beyond a regular group run. Lower uniqueness score because group runs happen regularly, but the official Global Running Day hook and city partnership elevate it slightly.

event_name: NFC Fight Night at Live! at The Battery Atlanta
venue: Live! at The Battery Atlanta, Atlanta
blurb: If you have never watched live MMA up close, this is a different thing entirely from watching it on TV. NFC Fight Night brings over 12 professional and amateur fights, including MMA, Muay Thai, kickboxing, and bare knuckle, to a 32-foot LED screen setup at Live! at The Battery. It starts at 5pm so there is plenty of night left after. It is a great call for anyone with a teenager who thinks they have seen it all, or honestly for any adult who wants a Saturday night that actually has some energy to it.
scoring_notes: Strong uniqueness and energy. The Battery location is very familiar and accessible to East Cobb readers. Appeals to male-skewing adults and teen-age kids. Not every reader, but a strong pick for the right segment. Combat sports events at this venue are not every weekend.
